chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitution Petition No. D-1712 of 2023 (Gul Mir Jatoi Vs. Federation of Pakistan & others) DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For Orders on office objection. 2. For Orders on CMA No. 7375/2023 (Ex./A) 3. For hearing of main case. 21-02-2024. Petitioner Gul Mir Jatoi in person. ******** 1. Over ruled. 2. Granted subject to all just legal exceptions. 3. By way of instant petition, it is stated by the petitioner that the respondents be directed to restore the electric supply to his house in terms of Ombudsman’s order and not to force him to deposit wrong bills. The SEPCO is the Company with non-existing rules, it is supplying the electricity to its customers on payment, if such supply is disconnected by the SEPCO then possibly it may be on account of non-payment of such bills. Even otherwise, the dispute with regard to issuance of bills towards consumption of electricity being wrong or genuine being factual, is not amendable to writ jurisdiction of this Court. In view of above, the instant petition being misconceived is dismissed in limine. Judge Judge Nasim/P.A
